name: Supermarine Spitfire

specs.powerplant: 1× Rolls-Royce Merlin V12
specs.armament: 8× .303 Browning machine guns

funFact: Over 20,000 Spitfires were built, and it was the only British fighter produced continuously throughout WWII.
blurb: The iconic British single-seat fighter whose elliptical wing and Merlin engine made it a symbol of the Battle of Britain.

name: Messerschmitt Bf 109

specs.powerplant: 1× Daimler-Benz DB 605 V12
specs.armament: 2× 13mm MG 131, 1× 20mm/30mm cannon

funFact: With nearly 34,000 built, it is the most-produced fighter aircraft in history.
blurb: Germany's principal fighter of WWII, flown by the highest-scoring aces in aviation history.

name: F-15 Eagle

specs.powerplant: 2× Pratt & Whitney F100 turbofans
specs.armament: 20mm M61 Vulcan, AIM-9, AIM-120

funFact: The F-15 has an air-to-air combat record of over 100 victories with zero losses.
blurb: A twin-engine all-weather air-superiority fighter designed to gain and maintain control of the skies.

name: P-38 Lightning

specs.powerplant: 2× Allison V-1710-111/113 turbosupercharged V12, 1,600 hp each
specs.armament: 1× 20mm Hispano cannon, 4× .50 cal Browning M2 machine guns

funFact: The P-38 was the only American fighter aircraft in production throughout the entire US involvement in WWII, and it was the aircraft in which America's top two aces, Richard Bong and Thomas McGuire, scored all their kills.
blurb: Lockheed's distinctive twin-boom, twin-engine fighter was one of the most versatile aircraft of WWII, excelling as a fighter, bomber escort, and reconnaissance platform. Its distinctive silhouette and long range made it the primary American fighter in the Pacific Theater.

name: P-47 Thunderbolt

specs.powerplant: 1× Pratt & Whitney R-2800-59 Double Wasp radial, 2,535 hp
specs.armament: 8× .50 cal Browning M2 machine guns, up to 1,100 kg bombs

funFact: The P-47 was one of the heaviest single-engine piston fighters of WWII, yet pilots credited its rugged construction with bringing them home after sustaining damage that would have destroyed other aircraft.
blurb: The massive Republic P-47 Thunderbolt was one of the main USAAF fighters of WWII, renowned for its toughness and ability to survive battle damage. Originally designed as a high-altitude escort, it evolved into a devastating ground-attack aircraft in the European and Pacific theaters.

name: P-51 Mustang

specs.powerplant: 1× Packard V-1650-7 Merlin V12, 1,490 hp
specs.armament: 6× .50 cal Browning M2 machine guns

funFact: When paired with drop tanks, the P-51D could escort B-17 bombers all the way to Berlin and back from England, fundamentally changing the strategic bombing campaign over Germany.
blurb: Widely considered the finest American fighter of WWII, the P-51 Mustang combined speed, range, and maneuverability in an airframe that could match the best German and Japanese fighters. Its introduction as a long-range escort fighter dramatically reduced bomber losses over Europe.

name: F-86 Sabre

specs.powerplant: 1× General Electric J47-GE-27 turbojet, 26.3 kN thrust
specs.armament: 6× .50 cal M3 Browning machine guns

funFact: The F-86 Sabre achieved a kill ratio of approximately 10:1 over the MiG-15 in Korea, though some historians debate these figures as Soviet and Chinese records suggest a ratio closer to 2:1.
blurb: America's first swept-wing jet fighter, the F-86 Sabre is best remembered for its legendary duels with the Soviet MiG-15 over 'MiG Alley' during the Korean War. It became the backbone of NATO air defenses throughout the early Cold War and was operated by over 30 nations.

name: F-100 Super Sabre

specs.powerplant: 1× Pratt & Whitney J57-P-21A afterburning turbojet, 75.4 kN thrust
specs.armament: 4× 20mm M39 cannon, up to 3,400 kg external stores

funFact: The F-100 Super Sabre was the world's first operational supersonic jet fighter capable of level supersonic flight, achieving Mach 1 in level flight on its first flight in May 1953.
blurb: The North American F-100 Super Sabre was the first of the USAF's 'Century Series' fighters and the first American aircraft capable of sustained supersonic flight in level flight. It served extensively in Vietnam primarily as a ground-attack aircraft and in the 'Wild Weasel' suppression of enemy air defenses role.

name: F-104 Starfighter

specs.powerplant: 1× General Electric J79-GE-11A afterburning turbojet, 70.3 kN thrust
specs.armament: 1× 20mm M61A1 Vulcan rotary cannon, AIM-9 Sidewinder missiles

funFact: The F-104 had the smallest wing of any operational fighter jet — just 6.63 m wingspan on a 16.7 m fuselage — giving it such poor low-speed handling that pilots called it 'the missile with a man in it.'
blurb: Designed by Kelly Johnson after consultations with Korean War fighter pilots who wanted pure speed, the F-104 Starfighter was a radical departure from conventional fighter design. Its razor-thin, stubby wings gave it outstanding high-altitude speed but also earned it a controversial safety record, particularly in West Germany where it was nicknamed 'the Widowmaker.'

name: F-4 Phantom II

specs.powerplant: 2× General Electric J79-GE-17A afterburning turbojets, 79.4 kN each
specs.armament: 1× 20mm M61A1 Vulcan cannon (F-4E), AIM-7 Sparrow, AIM-9 Sidewinder, up to 8,480 kg ordnance

funFact: The F-4 Phantom II was so capable that it served in the air forces of 11 countries, and some nations flew it into the 2010s. It holds the distinction of being the last US aircraft to produce an ace pilot (two MiG kills each needed for a five-kill ace) in Vietnam.
blurb: The McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II is one of the most iconic jet fighters ever built, serving the US Navy, Marines, and Air Force simultaneously — a rare achievement. Originally designed without a gun (relying solely on missiles), combat experience in Vietnam proved this to be a serious deficiency, leading to the addition of the M61 cannon in later variants.

name: F-14 Tomcat

specs.powerplant: 2× General Electric F110-GE-400 afterburning turbofans, 102.8 kN each
specs.armament: 1× 20mm M61A1 Vulcan cannon, AIM-54 Phoenix, AIM-7 Sparrow, AIM-9 Sidewinder

funFact: The F-14's AIM-54 Phoenix missile could engage targets at ranges exceeding 190 km, making it the longest-range air-to-air missile ever carried by a US fighter. The Tomcat also gained worldwide fame from the 1986 film Top Gun.
blurb: Grumman's swing-wing masterpiece, the F-14 Tomcat was designed to replace the F-4 Phantom as the US Navy's primary fleet defense interceptor. Its variable-geometry wings swept from 20° to 68°, optimizing performance across the entire flight envelope from carrier landing to Mach 2+ intercepts.

name: F-16 Fighting Falcon

specs.powerplant: 1× Pratt & Whitney F100-PW-229 afterburning turbofan, 129.4 kN thrust
specs.armament: 1× 20mm M61A1 Vulcan cannon, 11 hardpoints for AIM-9, AIM-120, bombs, and other ordnance

funFact: The F-16 was the first operational fighter aircraft to be deliberately designed to be aerodynamically unstable, relying on a fly-by-wire system to remain controllable — a design choice that dramatically improved its maneuverability.
blurb: The General Dynamics F-16 Fighting Falcon is the world's most prolific modern jet fighter, with over 4,600 built and operated by 25+ nations. Born from the Lightweight Fighter competition of the 1970s, it set new standards for agility and pilot ergonomics including the reclined seat and side-stick controller.

name: F/A-18 Hornet

specs.powerplant: 2× General Electric F404-GE-402 afterburning turbofans, 79.0 kN each
specs.armament: 1× 20mm M61A1 Vulcan cannon, AIM-9, AIM-7, AIM-120, AGM-65, JDAM, and other stores

funFact: The F/A-18 Hornet was the first aircraft to transition directly from an air-to-air engagement to an air-to-ground strike mission in a single sortie during the Gulf War, cementing its 'F/A' (Fighter/Attack) designation.
blurb: The McDonnell Douglas F/A-18 Hornet was the US Navy and Marine Corps' workhorse multirole aircraft for four decades. Derived from the YF-17 lightweight fighter prototype, it proved remarkably adaptable, serving as the mount for the Blue Angels demonstration team and seeing combat in every major US conflict from Panama to Afghanistan.

name: A-10 Thunderbolt II

specs.powerplant: 2× General Electric TF34-GE-100A turbofans, 40.3 kN each
specs.armament: 1× 30mm GAU-8/A Avenger 7-barrel rotary cannon, up to 7,260 kg ordnance on 11 hardpoints

funFact: The A-10 was designed around its gun — the GAU-8/A Avenger cannon is so large and powerful that the entire aircraft is essentially built around it, and the recoil from firing is significant enough to slow the aircraft.
blurb: The ungainly but beloved 'Warthog' was purpose-built for close air support of ground troops, designed to survive in the extremely hostile environment of low-altitude battlefield operations. Its titanium 'bathtub' cockpit armor, redundant systems, and ability to fly on a single engine have made it legendary for bringing pilots home after taking devastating battle damage.

name: F-22 Raptor

specs.powerplant: 2× Pratt & Whitney F119-PW-100 afterburning turbofans, 156 kN each
specs.armament: 1× 20mm M61A2 Vulcan cannon, AIM-9X Sidewinder, AIM-120 AMRAAM (internal bays)

funFact: The F-22 can supercruise — sustain supersonic flight without afterburner — at Mach 1.82, a capability that allows it to engage and disengage from combat on its own terms, something no previous fighter could do.
blurb: The Lockheed Martin F-22 Raptor is the world's first operational fifth-generation stealth air superiority fighter, combining stealth, supercruise, thrust vectoring, and advanced avionics in a way that no adversary can currently match. Only 187 were built due to cost, making it one of the most expensive fighters ever produced.

name: F-35 Lightning II

specs.powerplant: 1× Pratt & Whitney F135-PW-100 afterburning turbofan, 191 kN thrust
specs.armament: 1× 25mm GAU-22/A rotary cannon (F-35A, internal), AIM-9X, AIM-120, JDAM (internal and external hardpoints)

funFact: The F-35 program is the most expensive weapons system in history, with a projected lifetime cost exceeding $1.7 trillion. The F-35B variant can perform short takeoffs and vertical landings (STOVL), a rare combination at supersonic speeds.
blurb: The Lockheed Martin F-35 Lightning II is a family of stealth multirole fighters developed to replace a wide variety of legacy aircraft across the US military and allied nations. Its sensor fusion, stealth, and networking capabilities give it unprecedented situational awareness, making it as much an airborne command node as a fighter.

name: Hawker Hurricane

specs.powerplant: 1× Rolls-Royce Merlin XX V-12 liquid-cooled, 1,185 hp
specs.armament: 4× 20mm Hispano Mk.II cannon (Mk.IIc)

funFact: Although overshadowed by the Spitfire in legend, Hurricanes actually destroyed more enemy aircraft during the Battle of Britain than all other defences combined — claiming over 55% of Luftwaffe losses.
blurb: The Hawker Hurricane was the RAF's first monoplane fighter and its most numerous aircraft at the outbreak of WWII, playing a decisive role in the Battle of Britain in 1940. Rugged, stable, and easy to repair in the field, it was the workhorse of the RAF's fighter force across every major theatre — from the Western Desert to the Far East.

name: de Havilland Mosquito

specs.powerplant: 2× Rolls-Royce Merlin 23/25 V-12, 1,460 hp each
specs.armament: 4× 20mm Hispano cannon, 4× .303 Browning machine guns, up to 900 kg bombs (FB.VI)

funFact: Built almost entirely of wood — including a sandwich of balsa between birch plywood — the Mosquito was so fast when introduced that the RAF initially struggled to find a role for it, since it could outrun virtually every enemy aircraft.
blurb: The de Havilland Mosquito was one of WWII's most versatile and remarkable aircraft, excelling as a bomber, night fighter, photo-reconnaissance platform, and pathfinder. Its wooden construction, twin Merlin engines, and exceptional performance made it the fastest Allied aircraft for much of the war, enabling precision low-level raids deep into occupied Europe.

name: Gloster Meteor

specs.powerplant: 2× Rolls-Royce Derwent 8 centrifugal turbojets, 16.0 kN each
specs.armament: 4× 20mm Hispano Mk.V cannon in nose

funFact: The Meteor was the only Allied jet aircraft to see combat in WWII, and it holds a unique record of being the first jet aircraft to achieve an aerial victory — by ramming a V-1 flying bomb when its guns jammed.
blurb: The Gloster Meteor was Britain's first operational jet fighter and the only Allied jet to see combat in World War II, used primarily to intercept the V-1 flying bomb menace over southern England. As the most-produced Western jet of the late 1940s, it also served with distinction in the Korean War with Australia's RAAF.

name: Hawker Hunter

specs.powerplant: 1× Rolls-Royce Avon 207 turbojet, 45.1 kN thrust
specs.armament: 4× 30mm ADEN revolver cannon; up to 3,360 kg external stores (FGA.9)

funFact: In October 1953, test pilot Neville Duke set a world air speed record of 1,171 km/h (728 mph) in a Hunter prototype, briefly making it the fastest aircraft in the world.
blurb: The Hawker Hunter was the definitive British subsonic jet fighter of the 1950s — elegant, reliable, and widely exported to over 20 nations. Its exceptional handling and powerful cannon armament made it beloved by pilots, and its adaptability saw it serve in strike, trainer, and display roles into the 21st century.

name: English Electric Lightning

specs.powerplant: 2× Rolls-Royce Avon 301R afterburning turbojets (stacked vertically), 72.8 kN each
specs.armament: 2× 30mm ADEN cannon; 2× de Havilland Firestreak or Red Top IR missiles

funFact: The Lightning's engines are mounted one above the other in a unique stacked arrangement — a design that made it extraordinarily fast but gave it a notoriously short range, earning it the nickname 'the electric jet' for how quickly it burned through fuel.
blurb: The English Electric Lightning was Britain's only indigenous supersonic interceptor and the fastest aircraft the RAF ever operated, capable of climbing to 12,000 m in under three minutes. Its unique stacked-engine layout, Mach 2+ performance, and dramatic vertical climb ability made it a spectacular but fuel-hungry defender of UK airspace throughout the Cold War.

name: Hawker Siddeley Harrier

specs.powerplant: 1× Rolls-Royce Pegasus Mk.103 vectored-thrust turbofan, 95.6 kN thrust
specs.armament: 2× 30mm ADEN cannon pods; AIM-9 Sidewinder, bombs, rockets on underwing pylons

funFact: The Harrier was the world's first operational VTOL jet combat aircraft, and its ability to operate from forest clearings, roads, and ski-jump-equipped carriers fundamentally changed thinking about air power dispersal and survivability.
blurb: The Hawker Siddeley Harrier revolutionised military aviation as the world's first truly successful V/STOL combat jet, using a single vectored-thrust engine to take off and land vertically. Its finest hour came during the 1982 Falklands War, where Sea Harriers operating from small carriers in the South Atlantic defeated a modern Argentine air force without losing a single aircraft in air-to-air combat.

name: Panavia Tornado

specs.powerplant: 2× Turbo-Union RB199-34R Mk.103 afterburning turbofans, 76.8 kN each
specs.armament: 2× 27mm Mauser BK-27 cannon; up to 9,000 kg ordnance including Storm Shadow, Brimstone, Paveway

funFact: The Tornado was the first European combat aircraft designed from the outset for the specific mission of low-level supersonic penetration at night and in all weather — flying below radar cover at treetop height to strike Soviet airfields.
blurb: The Panavia Tornado was a landmark multinational European programme developed jointly by the UK, Germany, and Italy, combining a swing-wing design with the ability to sprint at Mach 2.2 or cruise efficiently at low level. It served as the backbone of NATO strike forces from the Gulf War through to its retirement by the RAF in 2019, constantly updated with precision weapons including Storm Shadow cruise missiles.

name: Eurofighter Typhoon

specs.powerplant: 2× Eurojet EJ200 afterburning turbofans, 90.0 kN each
specs.armament: 1× 27mm Mauser BK-27 cannon; AMRAAM, ASRAAM, Meteor, Brimstone, Paveway IV on 13 hardpoints

funFact: The Typhoon can supercruise at up to Mach 1.5 without afterburner, and in a 2008 exercise it defeated the F-22 Raptor in simulated close-range combat — though the F-22's designers argued beyond-visual-range would tell a different story.
blurb: The Eurofighter Typhoon is a collaborative 4.5-generation air superiority and multirole fighter built by a four-nation consortium (UK, Germany, Italy, and Spain), combining canard-delta aerodynamics with fly-by-wire instability for extreme agility. Operated by the RAF, Luftwaffe, and eight other air forces, it bridges the gap between Cold War fighters and fifth-generation stealth designs.

name: Focke-Wulf Fw 190

specs.powerplant: 1× BMW 801D-2 14-cylinder air-cooled radial, 1,700 hp
specs.armament: 2× 13mm MG 131 machine guns, 4× 20mm MG 151/20 cannon (A-8)

funFact: When the Fw 190 first appeared over the English Channel in late 1941, it so outclassed the Spitfire Mk.V that the RAF urgently captured an intact example — by accident, when a disoriented German pilot landed at a British airfield.
blurb: The Focke-Wulf Fw 190 was Germany's premier radial-engine fighter of WWII, and its arrival over France in 1941 shocked the RAF by outperforming the then-standard Spitfire Mk.V in almost every performance metric. Designed by Kurt Tank, it proved extraordinarily adaptable — serving as a pure fighter, fighter-bomber, close-support aircraft, and even torpedo carrier throughout the war.

name: Messerschmitt Me 262

specs.powerplant: 2× Junkers Jumo 004B-1 axial-flow turbojets, 8.8 kN each
specs.armament: 4× 30mm MK 108 cannon in nose; optional R4M rockets

funFact: The Me 262 was approximately 150 km/h faster than the best Allied piston fighters, and Luftwaffe pilots found that Allied aircraft could not catch it in level flight — only diving from altitude above gave Allied pilots any chance of interception.
blurb: The Messerschmitt Me 262 Schwalbe ('Swallow') was the world's first operational jet-powered fighter aircraft, entering Luftwaffe service in 1944 and representing a quantum leap in aviation technology. Despite its revolutionary performance advantage, Hitler's insistence on using it as a bomber rather than an interceptor, combined with chronic fuel shortages and pilot training difficulties, prevented it from decisively affecting the war's outcome.

name: Messerschmitt Me 163 Komet

specs.powerplant: 1× Walter HWK 509A-2 bi-fuel rocket motor, 16.7 kN thrust
specs.armament: 2× 30mm MK 108 cannon in wing roots

funFact: The Me 163 was not only the world's first and only operational rocket-powered fighter, but it was also the first piloted aircraft to exceed 1,000 km/h in level flight — a feat so startling that early Allied pilots who reported being attacked by it were initially disbelieved.
blurb: The Messerschmitt Me 163 Komet was the most radical fighter of WWII: a tailless rocket-powered interceptor that burned through its entire fuel supply in under eight minutes, then glided back to its base unpowered. Its extreme speed made it nearly impossible for Allied pilots to engage, but its hazardous propellants, violent landing characteristics, and window of vulnerability while gliding made it as dangerous to its own pilots as to the enemy.

name: Junkers Ju 87 Stuka

specs.powerplant: 1× Junkers Jumo 211D V-12 liquid-cooled, 1,200 hp
specs.armament: 3× 7.92mm MG 17/MG 81 machine guns; up to 1,800 kg bombs including 1× SC 500 under fuselage (B-2)

funFact: The Stuka's undercarriage was fitted with wind-driven sirens that produced its terrifying scream during dives — a deliberate psychological weapon designed to break enemy morale that proved enormously effective in 1939–41 campaigns.
blurb: The Junkers Ju 87 Stuka was the fearsome symbol of Nazi Blitzkrieg, its wailing siren and near-vertical dive attacks terrorising civilian and military targets across Poland, France, and the Balkans. Although vulnerable to fighter opposition — a fatal weakness exposed during the Battle of Britain — the Stuka continued to inflict devastating anti-tank damage on the Eastern Front, particularly under ace Hans-Ulrich Rudel who flew over 2,500 combat missions in the type.

name: Heinkel He 162

specs.powerplant: 1× BMW 003E-1 axial-flow turbojet, 7.8 kN thrust
specs.armament: 2× 20mm MG 151/20 cannon (A-2 variant)

funFact: The He 162 Volksjäger ('People's Fighter') was designed, built, and flown operationally in under 90 days — an almost incomprehensible engineering achievement — yet it was so hastily constructed from wood and metal that early production aircraft suffered structural failures in flight.
blurb: The Heinkel He 162 was a desperate last-ditch jet interceptor designed by Germany in late 1944 to be mass-produced using non-strategic materials and flown by Hitler Youth with minimal training. Despite its rushed development and structural problems, the tiny shoulder-mounted jet was genuinely fast and agile, though it arrived far too late and in too few numbers to influence the war's outcome.
